name: Kang-Ped Bhet-Yang - Thai Roast Duck Red Curr
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. Roast Duck
2. 5 Plum Tomatoes
3. 1/2 c  Small Thai Eggplants (about the size of large green peas)
4. 4 Fresh Medium Hot Chili Peppers
5. 4 Kaffir Lime Leaves
6. 1/2 bn Thai Basil (Horapha)
7. 1 tsp Coconut Sugar
8. 3 cups Coconut Cream
9. 5 cups Coconut Milk
10. Fish Sauce
-------------
1. Put the ingredients for the red curry paste into a mortar and pound until well mixed into a paste. You may use commercially available paste if available. Adjust to taste.
2. Debone the roast duck, and cut up the meat into bite-size rectangular pieces (leaving the skin on). Cut the neck and wing into pieces.
3. Place the coconut milk in a large saucepan and heat until boiling. Add duck pieces and cook until tender.
4. Put the coconut cream into a frying pan, add red curry paste. Heat over fairly high heat, stirring constantly, until all the paste has mixed in and is thoroughly heated. Add Kaffir Lime leaves, fish sauce to taste, and coconut sugar, and continue to heat (keep stirring all the time to prevent burning) until a light oil starts to form on the surface of the "sauce".
5. Add the "sauce" to the saucepan of duck and coconut milk, and bring back to a boil. Add all remaining ingredients except the basil, which is added when the curry starts to boil. Remove from heat and serve with plain boiled white rice.
